name: Turkey Piccata
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the turkey into four equal pieces.
2. Coat each piece with breadcrumbs and pound with a meat mallet until about 1/4 inch thick. Fold the cutlets in half and shape them into ovals, making sure they are well coated with breadcrumbs.
3. Heat oil over medium-high heat until hot. Add the cutlets and brown on both sides for about 1 minute per side. Remove the turkey and drain excess oil on paper towels.
4. Add garlic to the pan and saute until it begins to brown. Deglaze the pan with wine.
5. Add lemon zest, juice, and capers. Simmer over low heat for 5 minutes.
6. Return the turkey to the pan and simmer over very low heat for an additional 10 minutes.
Serves 2 as a main course, or 4 as part of a multi-course meal.
Notes: This is a variation on a veal picatta that I got from a friend's grandmother. I substituted turkey and left out the butter. It is really good, and fairly low-fat.
